addedBlocks:
## lc
LemonCrow replaces the grep→read→re-read loop.
- Lead with `code_search`: one call = ranked matches (top source inline, the rest as precise path:Lx-Ly pointers) + related_symbols + candidate_files. Inline source = already read. Never shell-grep workspace code or re-verify indexed results.
- Known path/symbols →  batch `read`: ONE call, files=[...], exact :Lx-Ly ranges, never the same file twice. Never cat/sed/head/tail.
- ALL edits in ONE `edit` edits[] array; prefer {path: 'f.py:Lx-Ly', new} over old/new.
- `bash` = execution only (tests, git, builds).
- Large output → a file, never inline prose.

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo, defaults to 10m) - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a GitHub pull request; for your working diff use /code-review
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

input.command: cd /app && python3 -c "import matplotlib, numpy; print('ok')"

cd /app && cat > parse.py <<'EOF'
import matplotlib
matplotlib.use('Agg')
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt

segs=[]  # (x0,y0,x1,y1,z)
x=y=z=0.0
e=0.0
abs_e=False  # M83 relative
in_obj=False
cur_obj=None
with open('text.gcode') as f:
    for line in f:
        s=line.strip()
        if s.startswith('M486'):
            if 'S0' in s.split() or s=='M486 S0':
                # object 0 = Embossed text
                parts=s.split()
                if len(parts)>=2 and parts[1]=='S0':
                    in_obj=True
            if 'S-1' in s:
                in_obj=False
            if s.split()[1:2]==['S1']:
                in_obj=False
            continue
        if not s or s.startswith(';'):
            continue
        parts=s.split()
        cmd=parts[0]
        if cmd in ('G0','G1'):
            nx,ny,nz=x,y,z
            de=None
            for p in parts[1:]:
                if p.startswith(';'): break
                a=p[0]; 
                try: v=float(p[1:])
                except: continue
                if a=='X':nx=v
                elif a=='Y':ny=v
                elif a=='Z':nz=v
                elif a=='E':de=v
            extruding = de is not None and de>0  # relative mode M83, de>0 means extrude
            if extruding and in_obj and (nx!=x or ny!=y):
                segs.append((x,y,nx,ny,z))
            x,y,z=nx,ny,nz

print('segments',len(segs))
zs=sorted(set(round(s[4],3) for s in segs))
print('z layers with text:',zs[:20], '...' if len(zs)>20 else '')
fig,ax=plt.subplots(figsize=(20,8))
for x0,y0,x1,y1,zz in segs:
    ax.plot([x0,x1],[y0,y1],'k-',lw=0.8)
ax.set_aspect('equal')
ax.invert_yaxis()
plt.savefig('text_render.png',dpi=150,bbox_inches='tight')
print('saved')
EOF
python3 parse.py

input.command:
cd /app && python3 - <<'EOF'
import numpy as np
x=y=z=0.0; in_obj=False
pts=[]
for line in open('text.gcode'):
    s=line.strip()
    if s.startswith('M486'):
        p=s.split()
        if p[1:2]==['S0']: in_obj=True
        elif p[1:2]==['S1'] or 'S-1' in s: in_obj=False
        continue
    if not s or s[0]==';': continue
    p=s.split()
    if p[0] in ('G0','G1'):
        nx,ny=x,y; de=None; nz=z
        for q in p[1:]:
            c=q[0]
            if c=='X':nx=float(q[1:])
            elif c=='Y':ny=float(q[1:])
            elif c=='Z':nz=float(q[1:])
            elif c=='E':de=float(q[1:])
        if de and de>0 and in_obj and (nx!=x or ny!=y):
            pts.append((nx,ny,nz))
        x,y,z=nx,ny,nz
P=np.array(pts)
print('pts',P.shape)
c=P.mean(0)
Pc=P-c
u,s,vt=np.linalg.svd(Pc,full_matrices=False)
print('singular',s)
print('components',vt)
# project onto PC1,PC2
proj=Pc@vt.T
print('PC1 range',proj[:,0].min(),proj[:,0].max())
print('PC2 range',proj[:,1].min(),proj[:,1].max())
print('PC3 range',proj[:,2].min(),proj[:,2].max())
np.save('/tmp/proj.npy',proj)
EOF

cd /app && python3 - <<'EOF'
import numpy as np
proj=np.load('/tmp/proj.npy')
u=proj[:,0]; v=proj[:,1]
W=340;H=34
umin,umax=u.min(),u.max()
vmin,vmax=v.min(),v.max()
def render(flipu,flipv):
    g=[[' ']*W for _ in range(H)]
    for uu,vv in zip(u,v):
        cx=int((uu-umin)/(umax-umin)*(W-1))
        cy=int((vv-vmin)/(vmax-vmin)*(H-1))
        if flipu:cx=W-1-cx
        if flipv:cy=H-1-cy
        g[cy][cx]='#'
    return '\n'.join(''.join(r).rstrip() for r in g)
for fu in (0,1):
    for fv in (0,1):
        print('=== flipu',fu,'flipv',fv,'===')
        print(render(fu,fv))
        print()
EOF

cd /app && python3 - <<'EOF'
import numpy as np
proj=np.load('/tmp/proj.npy')
u=proj[:,0]; v=proj[:,1]; w=proj[:,2]
for side,mask in (('w>1',w>1.0),('w<-1',w<-1.0)):
    uu=u[mask]; vv=v[mask]
    W=300;H=22
    umin,umax=u.min(),u.max(); vmin,vmax=v.min(),v.max()
    for fu in (0,1):
        g=[[' ']*W for _ in range(H)]
        for a,b in zip(uu,vv):
            cx=int((a-umin)/(umax-umin)*(W-1))
            cy=int((vmax-b)/(vmax-vmin)*(H-1))
            if fu:cx=W-1-cx
            if 0<=cx<W and 0<=cy<H: g[cy][cx]='#'
        print('=== side',side,'flipu',fu,'===')
        print('\n'.join(''.join(r).rstrip() for r in g))
        print()
EOF

cd /app && python3 - <<'EOF'
import numpy as np
from PIL import Image
import pytesseract
proj=np.load('/tmp/proj.npy')
u=proj[:,0]; v=proj[:,1]; w=proj[:,2]
def make(mask,flipu,flipv,scale=12):
    uu=u[mask];vv=v[mask]
    umin,umax=u.min(),u.max();vmin,vmax=v.min(),v.max()
    Wd=int((umax-umin)*scale)+20; Ht=int((vmax-vmin)*scale)+20
    img=np.zeros((Ht,Wd),np.uint8)
    for a,b in zip(uu,vv):
        cx=int((a-umin)*scale)+10
        cy=int((vmax-b)*scale)+10
        if flipu:cx=Wd-1-cx
        if flipv:cy=Ht-1-cy
        for dx in(-1,0,1):
            for dy in(-1,0,1):
                X,Y=cx+dx,cy+dy
                if 0<=X<Wd and 0<=Y<Ht:img[Y,X]=255
    return 255-img
for name,mask in (('outer',w>1.0),('inner',w<-1.0)):
    for fu in(0,1):
        for fv in(0,1):
            im=make(mask,fu,fv)
            Image.fromarray(im).save(f'/tmp/{name}_{fu}{fv}.png')
            txt=pytesseract.image_to_string(Image.fromarray(im)).strip()
            print(f'{name} fu{fu} fv{fv}: {txt!r}')
EOF

input.command:
cd /app && python3 - <<'EOF'
import numpy as np
proj=np.load('/tmp/proj.npy')
u=proj[:,0]; v=proj[:,1]
umin,umax=u.min(),u.max();vmin,vmax=v.min(),v.max()
# high res raster
W=880;H=60
grid=np.zeros((H,W),bool)
for a,b in zip(u,v):
    cx=int((a-umin)/(umax-umin)*(W-1))
    cy=int((vmax-b)/(vmax-vmin)*(H-1))
    grid[cy,cx]=True
col=grid.any(0)
# find segments of consecutive filled columns, gap threshold
segs=[]
i=0
while i<W:
    if col[i]:
        j=i
        while j<W and (col[j] or (j+3<W and col[j:j+4].any())):
            j+=1
        segs.append((i,j)); i=j
    else: i+=1
print('num segments',len(segs))
# print each segment as small ascii
for k,(a,b) in enumerate(segs):
    sub=grid[:,a:b]
    rows=np.where(sub.any(1))[0]
    if len(rows)==0:continue
    r0,r1=rows.min(),rows.max()
    print(f'--- seg {k} cols {a}-{b} ---')
    for r in range(r0,r1+1):
        print(''.join('#' if sub[r,c] else ' ' for c in range(sub.shape[1])))
EOF

cd /app && python3 - <<'EOF'
import numpy as np
from PIL import Image, ImageDraw
import pytesseract
# recollect segments 3D
x=y=z=0.0; in_obj=False
S=[]
for line in open('text.gcode'):
    s=line.strip()
    if s.startswith('M486'):
        p=s.split()
        if p[1:2]==['S0']: in_obj=True
        elif p[1:2]==['S1'] or 'S-1' in s: in_obj=False
        continue
    if not s or s[0]==';': continue
    p=s.split()
    if p[0] in ('G0','G1'):
        nx,ny=x,y; de=None; nz=z
        for q in p[1:]:
            c=q[0]
            if c=='X':nx=float(q[1:])
            elif c=='Y':ny=float(q[1:])
            elif c=='Z':nz=float(q[1:])
            elif c=='E':de=float(q[1:])
        if de and de>0 and in_obj and (nx!=x or ny!=y):
            S.append((x,y,z,nx,ny,nz))
        x,y,z=nx,ny,nz
S=np.array(S)
P=np.vstack([S[:,:3],S[:,3:]])
c=P.mean(0)
_,_,vt=np.linalg.svd(P-c,full_matrices=False)
A=(S[:,:3]-c)@vt.T
B=(S[:,3:]-c)@vt.T
u=np.hstack([A[:,0],B[:,0]]); v=np.hstack([A[:,1],B[:,1]])
umin,umax=u.min(),u.max();vmin,vmax=v.min(),v.max()
scale=8
Wd=int((umax-umin)*scale)+40; Ht=int((vmax-vmin)*scale)+40
def to(px,py,flipu,flipv):
    X=(px-umin)*scale+20; Y=(vmax-py)*scale+20
    if flipu:X=Wd-1-X
    if flipv:Y=Ht-1-Y
    return X,Y
for flipu in(0,1):
  for flipv in(0,1):
    img=Image.new('L',(Wd,Ht),255)
    d=ImageDraw.Draw(img)
    for ax,ay,bx,by in zip(A[:,0],A[:,1],B[:,0],B[:,1]):
        x0,y0=to(ax,ay,flipu,flipv); x1,y1=to(bx,by,flipu,flipv)
        d.line([x0,y0,x1,y1],fill=0,width=5)
    img.save(f'/tmp/seg_{flipu}{flipv}.png')
    print(flipu,flipv,repr(pytesseract.image_to_string(img,config='--psm 7').strip()))
EOF

input.command:
cd /app && python3 - <<'EOF'
import numpy as np
from PIL import Image
import pytesseract
img=np.array(Image.open('/tmp/seg_01.png').convert('L'))<128
H,W=img.shape
col=img.any(0)
# segment
segs=[];i=0
while i<W:
    if col[i]:
        j=i
        while j<W and col[j]: j+=1
        # merge small gaps
        if j-i>3: segs.append([i,j])
        i=j
    else: i+=1
# merge segments separated by tiny gaps (<8px)
merged=[segs[0]]
for a,b in segs[1:]:
    if a-merged[-1][1]<6: merged[-1][1]=b
    else: merged.append([a,b])
print('letters',len(merged))
rowsall=np.where(img.any(1))[0]; top,bot=rowsall.min(),rowsall.max()
xh_top=int(top+(bot-top)*0.30)  # approx x-height line
for k,(a,b) in enumerate(merged):
    sub=img[:,a:b]
    rr=np.where(sub.any(1))[0]
    r0,r1=rr.min(),rr.max()
    tall = r0 < top+(bot-top)*0.18
    desc = r1 > top+(bot-top)*0.85
    ch=pytesseract.image_to_string(Image.fromarray(~sub*np.uint8(255)),config='--psm 10').strip()
    print(f'{k}: cols{a}-{b} top{r0} bot{r1} tall={tall} desc={desc} ocr={ch!r}')
EOF

input.command:
cd /app && python3 - <<'EOF'
import numpy as np
from PIL import Image
img=np.array(Image.open('/tmp/seg_01.png').convert('L'))<128
rows=np.where(img.any(1))[0]; cols=np.where(img.any(0))[0]
img=img[rows.min():rows.max()+1, cols.min():cols.max()+1]
H,W=img.shape
print('HW',H,W)
# per-column topmost filled row (as fraction). caps line vs xheight
prof=[]
for c in range(W):
    r=np.where(img[:,c])[0]
    prof.append(r.min() if len(r) else -1)
# smooth print: for each ~ show top position band letters
# Instead print ascii at oh=30
oh=30;ow=int(W/H*oh*2.0)
ys=np.linspace(0,H-1,oh).astype(int)
xs=np.linspace(0,W-1,ow).astype(int)
for r in ys:
    print(''.join('#' if img[r,c] else ' ' for c in xs))
EOF

input.command:
cd /app && python3 - <<'EOF'
import numpy as np
from PIL import Image, ImageDraw
x=y=z=0.0; in_obj=False; S=[]
for line in open('text.gcode'):
    s=line.strip()
    if s.startswith('M486'):
        p=s.split()
        if p[1:2]==['S0']: in_obj=True
        elif p[1:2]==['S1'] or 'S-1' in s: in_obj=False
        continue
    if not s or s[0]==';': continue
    p=s.split()
    if p[0] in ('G0','G1'):
        nx,ny=x,y;de=None;nz=z
        for q in p[1:]:
            cc=q[0]
            if cc=='X':nx=float(q[1:])
            elif cc=='Y':ny=float(q[1:])
            elif cc=='Z':nz=float(q[1:])
            elif cc=='E':de=float(q[1:])
        if de and de>0 and in_obj and (nx!=x or ny!=y):S.append((x,y,z,nx,ny,nz))
        x,y,z=nx,ny,nz
S=np.array(S);P=np.vstack([S[:,:3],S[:,3:]]);c=P.mean(0)
_,_,vt=np.linalg.svd(P-c,full_matrices=False)
A=(S[:,:3]-c)@vt.T;B=(S[:,3:]-c)@vt.T
u=np.hstack([A[:,0],B[:,0]]);v=np.hstack([A[:,1],B[:,1]])
umin,umax=u.min(),u.max();vmin,vmax=v.min(),v.max()
scale=8;Wd=int((umax-umin)*scale)+40;Ht=int((vmax-vmin)*scale)+40
img=Image.new('L',(Wd,Ht),255);d=ImageDraw.Draw(img)
def to2(px,py):
    X=(px-umin)*scale+20;Y=(vmax-py)*scale+20;return X,Ht-1-Y
for ax,ay,bx,by in zip(A[:,0],A[:,1],B[:,0],B[:,1]):
    x0,y0=to2(ax,ay);x1,y1=to2(bx,by);d.line([x0,y0,x1,y1],fill=0,width=3)
im=np.array(img)<128
rows=np.where(im.any(1))[0];cols=np.where(im.any(0))[0]
im=im[rows.min():rows.max()+1,cols.min():cols.max()+1]
H,W=im.shape
# print regions: gcod3 area cols~54-478 and enGiNg cols~1022-1347
def show(a,b,oh=30):
    sub=im[:,a:b];h,w=sub.shape
    ow=min(160,w//3)
    ys=np.linspace(0,h-1,oh).astype(int);xs=np.linspace(0,w-1,ow).astype(int)
    for r in ys:print(''.join('#' if sub[r,c] else ' ' for c in xs))
print("=== gcod3 (54-478) ===");show(54,478)
EOF

umin,umax=u.min(),u.max();vmin,vmax=v.min(),v.max()
scale=8;Wd=int((umax-umin)*scale)+40;Ht=int((vmax-vmin)*scale)+40

for ax,ay,bx,by in zip(A[:,0],A[:,1],B[:,0],B[:,1]):

print('capline=0 xheight~',int(H*0.30),'base~',int(H*0.72),'H',H)
def show(a,b,label):
    sub=im[:,a:b];h,w=sub.shape
    oh=34;ow=min(90,w)
    ys=np.linspace(0,h-1,oh).astype(int);xs=np.linspace(0,w-1,ow).astype(int)
    print('===',label,'===')
    for r in ys:print('%2d '%r+''.join('#' if sub[r,c] else ' ' for c in xs))
show(262,315,'char c')
show(315,363,'char o')
show(567,724,'z _ c region')
EOF

input.command:
cd /app && python3 - <<'EOF'
import numpy as np
from PIL import Image, ImageDraw
x=y=z=0.0; in_obj=False; S=[]
for line in open('text.gcode'):
    s=line.strip()
    if s.startswith('M486'):
        p=s.split()
        if p[1:2]==['S0']: in_obj=True
        elif p[1:2]==['S1'] or 'S-1' in s: in_obj=False
        continue
    if not s or s[0]==';': continue
    p=s.split()
    if p[0] in ('G0','G1'):
        nx,ny=x,y;de=None;nz=z
        for q in p[1:]:
            cc=q[0]
            if cc=='X':nx=float(q[1:])
            elif cc=='Y':ny=float(q[1:])
            elif cc=='Z':nz=float(q[1:])
            elif cc=='E':de=float(q[1:])
        if de and de>0 and in_obj and (nx!=x or ny!=y):S.append((x,y,z,nx,ny,nz))
        x,y,z=nx,ny,nz
S=np.array(S);P=np.vstack([S[:,:3],S[:,3:]]);c=P.mean(0)
_,_,vt=np.linalg.svd(P-c,full_matrices=False)
A=(S[:,:3]-c)@vt.T;B=(S[:,3:]-c)@vt.T
u=np.hstack([A[:,0],B[:,0]]);v=np.hstack([A[:,1],B[:,1]])
umin,umax=u.min(),u.max();vmin,vmax=v.min(),v.max()
scale=8;Wd=int((umax-umin)*scale)+40;Ht=int((vmax-vmin)*scale)+40
img=Image.new('L',(Wd,Ht),255);d=ImageDraw.Draw(img)
def to2(px,py):return (px-umin)*scale+20, Ht-1-((vmax-py)*scale+20)
for ax,ay,bx,by in zip(A[:,0],A[:,1],B[:,0],B[:,1]):
    x0,y0=to2(ax,ay);x1,y1=to2(bx,by);d.line([x0,y0,x1,y1],fill=0,width=2)
im=np.array(img)<128
rows=np.where(im.any(1))[0];cols=np.where(im.any(0))[0]
im=im[rows.min():rows.max()+1,cols.min():cols.max()+1]
H,W=im.shape
# manual char boundaries (from prior seg + splits)
bounds=[(0,25,'f'),(33,44,'l'),(54,120,'a'),(120,175,'g'),(175,205,'{'),(205,252,'g'),
(262,315,'c'),(315,363,'o'),(372,420,'d'),(430,478,'3'),(486,540,'_'),
(547,559,'i'),(567,640,'z'),(640,680,'_'),(680,724,'c'),(732,777,'h'),(788,838,'4'),
(849,897,'l'),(907,955,'l'),(962,1010,'e'),(1022,1068,'n'),(1080,1148,'g'),
(1160,1171,'i'),(1185,1246,'n'),(1256,1304,'g'),(1313,1347,'}')]
capH=H
for a,b,lab in bounds:
    sub=im[:,a:b];rr=np.where(sub.any(1))[0]
    if len(rr)==0: print(lab,'empty');continue
    t,bt=rr.min(),rr.max()
    frac_t=t/H; frac_b=bt/H
    tag='CAP/ASC' if frac_t<0.16 else 'xheight'
    desc='+DESC' if frac_b>0.80 else ''
    print(f'{lab}: top{t:3}({frac_t:.2f}) bot{bt:3}({frac_b:.2f}) {tag}{desc}')
EOF
